channel.display-name: Weird or What? With William Shatner
The Yellow Couch with Jeremy Lynch
A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: Legend of the Lost
programme.desc: Told against the sweeping panoramas of the Sahara desert, this epic features an exotic mix of action, suspense, and romance and delivers a caravan of excitement - with screen icons John Wayne and Sophia Loren fighting to survive an arduous journey!

programme.title: Hart's War
programme.desc: War hero Col. McNamara is imprisoned in a German POW camp. As the camp's highest-ranking American officer, he commands his fellow inmates. Never giving up the fight to win the war, McNamara is planning, waiting for his moment to strike back at the enemy.

programme.title: Dead Man Walking
programme.desc: Inspired by the true story of a nun's relationship with a condemned man, this provocative examination of crime, punishment and redemption earned Susan Sarandon the 1995 Oscar for Best Actress and Sean Penn an Oscar nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: Drive
programme.desc: A Hollywood stunt performer (Ryan Gosling) who moonlights as a wheelman discovers that a contract has been put on him after a heist gone wrong.

programme.title: Jumper
programme.desc: A man discovers that he possesses the ability to instantly teleport anywhere in the world.

programme.title: Sky Kids
programme.desc: As a pair of schoolboys sneak a look around an empty private aircraft, two criminals take off with the kids still inside. Upon discovering the men are dangerous gangsters, the horrified lads inadvertently become embroiled in the mobsters's scheme.

programme.title: Next Goal Wins
programme.desc: With the 2014 World Cup fast approaching, Dutch football coach Thomas Rongen is hired to lead the famously pitiful national team of American Samoa. But despite the odds, can Rongen pave the way to the island’s first ever victory on the world stage?

programme.title: The Sentinel
programme.desc: Compromised by his affair with the first lady, a veteran secret service agent is framed as a potential presidential assassin and must rely on a hostile former prot‚g‚ to flush out the real culprit.

programme.title: Queen Of The Desert
programme.desc: A chronicle of Gertrude Bell's life, a traveler, writer, archaeologist, explorer, cartographer, and political attaché for the British Empire at the dawn of the twentieth century.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: When the Metajets discover a sunken prototype of the A-R-C Carrier in an unstable chasm under the ocean, the race is on to board the vessel and retrieve an important computer disk

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Gore at War
programme.desc: Making a comeback is what Gore does best, but will his finisher be enough to vanquish Varis?

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Eclipsalypso / Apocalypse Squirt
programme.desc: McGee summons the Moon Dervish who will bring the Apocalypse unless a sasquatch dances for him.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Make way for the newest team of aerial super-stars: The Sky-Riders!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Tony returns to Earth through the Naroomi vortex relic in order to find the Core Dreamstone, while Edyn, Strag and the Magi Resistance Force battle Agram on the drained seabed of Orothe.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Down and out, losing all but one of the Guardian Hyrens they worked so hard to collect, the Final Dreamers rush to Cald to face off against Gorgram, and his army of Shadow Magi.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: When an astronaut/scientist discovers an alien embryo in a strange alien pod, he asks the celebrated Dr. Piranoid to come up to the space station to examine it -- not knowing that Piranoid plans to gene-slam Manta-ray DNA with alien DNA.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Come on down to Camp PoopyKaka, Master Mayhem’s new summer camp! It’s free, fun, and you can never leave! The golden overlord has enlisted his monsters as camp counselors, while Major Disappointment roasts planet Earth like a marshmallow.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Drift
programme.desc: Thrust flees into the depths of Cybertron, pursued by Hot Shot and Wheeljack - and, separately, Starscream, who saves the children from being killed. The children discover the origins of the minicons.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Conspiracy
programme.desc: With the loss of the Star Sabre, the Autobots keep losing battles. In a fog-bound valley, Megatron plots to get the Sabre from Starscream, at the same time boosting his crew's confidence in himself. Scavenger's secret is revealed.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Come on down to Camp PoopyKaka, Master Mayhem’s new summer camp! It’s free, fun, and you can never leave! The golden overlord has enlisted his monsters as camp counselors, while Major Disappointment roasts planet Earth like a marshmallow.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Linkup
programme.desc: With Optimus damaged and temporarily out of commission, the newly-arrived Jetfire takes command - and seriously underestimates the Decepticons. But on the verge of defeat, Optimus reveals a new ability.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The opening day race for the new A-R-C racing season is threatened when the villainous Black Cloud - under the leadership of General Raven - kidnaps Maggie Strong, daughter of league chairman George Strong.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Chase
programme.desc: Trapped in cyberspace with Sideways, Alexis, Rad, Carlos and their minicons become aware of a massive evil entity, far more powerful and dangerous than Megatron, lurking threateningly in the background. Or was it all a dream?

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: The kids find themselves in Paradwyn, and reunite with another familiar face: Myrv, the Paradwynian scout. But they are shocked to discover that Myrv is able to magine a Hyren…and is able to keep it magined - something that they are unable to do.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B
programme.desc: Gretchen, McGee and Squirt get trapped by witch who forces the campers into pizza slave labour.
programme.sub-title: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: After failing to destroy the Earth yet again, Master Mayhem gathers his Monsters in the locker room for an evil pep talk. As they study their most fantastic fails, an ancient rivalry between RoBro and Teensy erupts!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: The Knights of Hanoi have set their sights on stealing Playmaker’s AI – but he won’t give it up without a fight!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Lakebo-tron / Rise of the Dawn of the Beginning of the Planet of Armand!
programme.desc: McGee and Buttsquat go head-to-head in an 80's style arcade game! But as the points build up, the boys become digitized inside the game. The kids head back through the Lakebottom inter-dimensional portal, hoping to find a Sasquatch version of the camp.
programme.sub-title: Lakebo-tron / Rise of the Dawn of the Beginning of the Planet of Armand!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: A comical dilemma involving Tony accidentally gathering together a herd of unwieldy creatures is solved by Edyn casting a spell. Tony and Strag wonder if they've been relying on Edyn's spells too much to solve their problems, but Edyn isn’t worried.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream
programme.desc: The gang finds themselves in the middle of a good ol' fashioned show down!
programme.sub-title: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Standing Tall
programme.desc: The Tower of Hanoi is nearly complete – leaving Varis a win away from deleting LINK VRAINS once and for all!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus
programme.desc: No one believes McGee's suspicion that the suddenly ultra-cool Buttsquat is a campyre.
programme.sub-title: Bite of the Buttsquat / Sword of Ittibiticus

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Jagged rocks and thick clouds surround them, villainous Murk Raiders are on their tail and an entire Cyclonian fleet waits for them beyond. The Storm Hawks have to fly silent and fly quick if they’re going to survive Terra Deep.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Kane’s irritation with all things modern has left him pining for the good ol’ days when everything was made of rock. And when something as simple as mashing potatoes becomes a complicated mess he finally snaps and takes off for some ‘alone time’.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Past, Part 1
programme.desc: Enthusiastic young Sideswipe, Blurr's hero-worshipper, joins the team and Optimus tasks Hot Shot with training him. Wheeljack also arrives on earth, bent on revenge for Hot Shot seemingly abandoning him during a battle on Cybertron.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slaybells / Smells Like Holidays
programme.desc: The kids almost ruin the biggest monster holiday of the year!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: In the fantastical world of the Moonlands, a pair of heroic Magi, Aidan and Elle, battle Agram, a villainous Shadow Magi. Utilizing the Dreamstones in the cover of the Book of Elders, they form the Core Glyph, a magical entrapment that locks Agram away.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Jet is wake-boarding across Lake Megalopolis when he does an aerial move, and tries to land… only to crash into a dry lake bed. All of the lake’s water has suddenly vanished, leaving behind only fish and the speedboat Chow was using to tow Jet.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Carnival
programme.desc: Rad and Carlos smuggle their minicons out of the base to visit the town's carnival. Billy and Fred see them transform and try to capture one, but are thwarted by Hot Shot.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The return of the deadly swarm takes a twist when the team discovers Dr. Lucas, the swarm’s creator, calling for help from within the swarm itself.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Link into the VRAINS
programme.desc: When a nefarious group of hackers threatens to destroy the cyber world, one duelist is determined to stop them – Playmaker!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Skor’s plan to put the Mayor of Megalopolis in a frilly dress by blasting him with his newly invented ‘Dressifier’ comes to a quick halt when the dinos intervene. But this defeat stings Skor’s ego more than usual.
programme.sub-title: Breaking Up Is Hard To Do

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Portent
programme.desc: Thrust joins Sideways by passing through a portal deep underground: his pursuers follow and find themselves on Cybertron's moon, which begins to disintegrate. To everyone's horror, Unicron is revealed.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: The duel between Playmaker and Spectre comes to a crashing conclusion as the bridge they’re battling on begins to break apart!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: The Final Hyren quest leads the kids to the Underneath, where they are shocked to find the realm being controlled by an evil Emperor. Strag discovers a secret even more horrible: his father is helping to rule the realm…

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Edgar volunteers to lead a Science Club at the local middle school. Seeking to impress the young students, he promises to bring in a “real dinosaur” for Show and Tell.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Hollow
programme.desc: Playmaker must cut down Spectre’s defenses if he is to topple the Tower of Hanoi!

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Montreal
programme.desc: Season XIV of the World Poker Tour continues at Playground Poker Club with WPT Montreal.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Montreal Part 2 of 3
programme.desc: Season XIV of the World Poker Tour continues at Playground Poker Club with WPT Montreal. Jared Mahoney started the final table last in chips, but has ascended into the chip lead.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Montreal Part 3 of 3
programme.desc: Season XIV of the World Poker Tour continues at Playground Poker Club with WPT Montreal.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: Five Diamond World Poker Classic
programme.desc: Season XIV of the World Poker Tour continues at the luxurious Bellagio in Las Vegas, where WPT champion and chip leader Kevin Eyster competes against Jake Schwartz.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Cycle 2 Week 6
programme.desc: The remaining models undergo a heavy metallic make-up photo shoot with John Swanell. Lisa arrives at the house and the girls have to critique each other, which causes even more fights. A practical joke gets taken the wrong way.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Cycle 2 Week 7
programme.desc: The girls shoot a thirty second jewellery promotion with top promo director Jason Clifford. Lucy, last years winner, visits the girls and gives them some tips. They have a country life photo shoot and there is a shock at elimination.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Cycle 2 Week 8
programme.desc: With two people gone the house becomes even more divided. The girls meet Saffron Aldridge, top style expert, who goes through their wardrobes with no mercy. They have a one on one with Lisa and the girls are awe-struck when they meet a famous model.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Cycle 2 Week 9
programme.desc: The four remaining models head to Morocco and Lisa gives them the day off. They are given the challenge of finding an outfit for Lisa. They have their first Moroccan photo shoot and the final three are chosen.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Cycle 2 Week 10
programme.desc: In the finale the three remaining models have their final photo shoot and the final two compete in a catwalk show and one girl is crowned 'Britain's Next Top Model'.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model: Episode 1
programme.desc: The girls immediately face their first challenge, posing in front of the press. They then meet stylist Hilary Alexander, who asks them when they lost their virginity and what they think about unprotected sex.

programme.title: Ex On The Beach: Double Trouble
programme.desc: The dating continues as Bobby and Bayley have a heart to heart. Arron's head gets turned but will he stray from Daisy? New arrivals rock the villa to its core and someone has to leave. (S9, ep2)

programme.title: Ex On The Beach: Heartbreak And Happiness
programme.desc: Aaron breaks away from Daisy but can he win over Dominika? A new Ex arrives and threatens to destroy Bobby and Bayley's relationship. (S9, ep3)

programme.title: Ex On The Beach: Double Departure
programme.desc: Bobby is left heartbroken by Bayley and Daisy is only too happy to pick up the pieces. Aaron continues to pursue Dominika and a VIP arrival brings drama to the villa. (S9, ep4)

programme.title: Ex On The Beach: Daisy's Disaster
programme.desc: Will a new arrival spell happiness for Josiah? There's a double blow for Daisy as she's left devastated by her ex and her friendship with the girls is left in tatters. (S9, ep5)

programme.title: Ex On The Beach: Bobby's Revenge
programme.desc: A new arrival spells trouble for George, giving Bobby a chance for revenge. Natalee seeks the green light from Daisy to crack on with Matty and Aaron could be playing with fire on a date. (S9, ep6)

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 12
programme.desc: Sketch is monkeying around with Jenny, Joe's Gary Lineker tat gets the red card and Alice fixes Sannahs unfortunate inking error.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 13
programme.desc: Emma needs Jay's help with a warning sign. Alice assists Louise with her eye-popping ink.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 14
programme.desc: Our artists make Nicole's bum look less laddy, save teacher loving George from detention, and stop Joanne getting spanked. Distraction desiring Reece, Turkish offender John and gardening lover James also get an inkjection.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 15
programme.desc: Nathan banishes his bad luck back tatt. Ollie's wheezy Magaluf inking is sent into orbit.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Tattoo Fixers - Xmas Special
programme.desc: The Tattoo Fixers are going all Santa by giving the nations' worst tattoo disasters the cover ups they desperately need.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 1
programme.desc: Jay banishes Ant's backside buddy, and Alice helps Chelsea get over the shock of her life.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 2
programme.desc: Sketch slays Fiona's zombie, while Glen gives magician George a frightening new friend.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 3
programme.desc: Sketch covers Chris's boastful nickname, and Glen removes Darren's chat up line leg art.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 192
programme.desc: Can the swinging-cage rescue in "Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest" really be done? Can an oil drum filled with methanol work as a high speed go kart?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 160
programme.desc: Does a certain 1970s sports car become more aerodynamic if the body is turned backwards? Can a surfboard be launched through the windscreen of a car and be lethal, like in the movie "Lethal Weapon 2?"

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 161
programme.desc: Can a motorcycle pull a tablecloth out from under a setting for a banquet? Do humans really use only 10% of their brain?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 193
programme.desc: Does a driver's choice of footwear affect driving performance? Does a full bladder pose as much of a driving hazard as being legally intoxicated? Can superglue be used to turn a room upside down?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 65
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a working glider be made out of concrete? Can an engine run on gunpowder alone?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 42
programme.desc: Myths tested: Could the Ancient Chinese have used drums to listen for tunneling invaders? Is the five second rule true?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 62
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a machine designed by Nikola Tesla actually create a miniature earthquake or collapse a structure? Can a lava lamp explode if heated on the stove?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 103
programme.desc: Myths tested: Did the Confederates really build a lethal steam-powered machine gun? Is it possible to fool a modern polygraph test?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 83
programme.desc: Myths tested: How can someone escape from a sinking car? Can paper be folded in half more than seven times?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 145
programme.desc: Can you accidentally kill yourself with a bullet that ricochets off three walls? Could a medieval army have used trees to catapult dead bodies over a castle wall?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 59
programme.desc: Myths tested: How easy is it to crack a safe? Can a hole be easily cut into a pane of glass without setting off a sound alarm?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 51
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a cell phone cause a plane to crash? Can a person fly away on a raft filled with helium?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 217
programme.desc: Can Adam and Jamie cross a canyon and reach civilization using only basic supplies, duct tape and bubble wrap?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 13
programme.desc: Myths tested: Could the sinking Titanic suck a person down with it? Does a goldfish's memory only last for seconds? Can explosives packed in a trombone cause the slide to rocket off it?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 113
programme.desc: Can a ninja catch an arrow mid-flight? Could a ninja hide underwater breathing through a bamboo reed long enough to attack an enemy with a poison blow-dart?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 49
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a football fly farther if it is filled with helium? Is it possible for a human to catch a bullet in his teeth?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 132
programme.desc: Could thermite placed on top of blocks of ice set off an explosion? Could a car stereo set off an SKS rifle? Could a person's thumb be severed while firing a hunting pistol?

programme.title: Mortdecai
programme.desc: Depp, Paltrow, and McGregor star in this globe-trotting comedy about an unlikely team recruited to find a stolen, priceless painting.

programme.title: The Hustle
programme.desc: Two incorrigible scam artists -- one low rent and the other high class -- compete to swindle a naïve tech prodigy out of his vast fortune.

programme.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels
programme.desc: The south of France. Two rival con men -- one is suave and sophisticated, the other is a third-rate American hustler -- make a wager.

programme.title: Love & Other Drugs
programme.desc: A womanizing Viagra salesman with no sense of direction or responsibility falls in love with a free-spirited woman.

programme.title: Super
programme.desc: After his wife falls under the influence of a drug dealer, an everyday guy transforms himself into Crimson Bolt, a superhero with the best intentions, but lacking in heroic skills.

programme.title: Scoop
programme.desc: An American journalism student in London scoops a big story, and begins an affair with an aristocrat as the incident unfurls.

programme.title: The Very Excellent Mr. Dundee
programme.desc: Paul Hogan, a retired actor who is constantly overshadowed by his character Crocodile Dundee, is reluctantly thrust back into the spotlight as he attempts to restore his sullied reputation on the eve of being awarded a knighthood by the Queen of England.

programme.title: Bandidas
programme.desc: In turn-of-the-century Mexico, two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their town.

programme.title: Next Goal Wins
programme.desc: With the 2014 World Cup fast approaching, Dutch football coach Thomas Rongen is hired to lead the famously pitiful national team of American Samoa. But despite the odds, can Rongen pave the way to the island’s first ever victory on the world stage?

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Inspector's Club': the Inspector accepts a challenge to solve the mystery of the mythical lost city of Atlantis. 'A Royal Pain': Bellhop Pinky stands in for a visiting 10-year-old king at a King's Convention and nearly starts World War III.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'All For Pink And Pink For All': Pinky, the Ant and Aardvark must save the Queen from the clutches of the evil Richelieu. 'Service With A Pink': An unscrupulous hotel boss forces Bellboy Panther to enter the wrestling ring with a vicious wrestler.
programme.sub-title: All For Pink And Pink For All/Service With A Pink Smile

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: The Pink The Bad And The Ugly
programme.desc: Sheriff Pink and Hoarse must rescue the town's money from Bandit Big Nose and his gang.
programme.sub-title: The Pink The Bad And The Ugly

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold had to the China seas to look for Professor Squakencluck who, despite having invented an object that can never be lost, ends up dropping the item in a remote island and getting lost himself.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Ghost of McCastle McDuckula
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny, on holiday in Scotland hope to stay at the Glen Sparrows Hotel, but Igor has other ideas.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Masquerading as an evil giant, VENOM sets up a giant magnetic force on a tropical island which pulls a freighter loaded with platinum right onto shore.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K teams Danger Mouse up with a robot Penfold, and Penfold with a robot Danger Mouse. The robots prove to be a lot of trouble for the real ones, taking instructions too literally or getting stuck running in circles.
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better Than Two

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to get Danger Mouse to bring him four ingredients needed for an evil spell to take over the world: The hair of a yeti, straw from a witch's broom, a piece of the London fog monster, and the tail feathers of Count Duckula.
programme.sub-title: The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM has a gravity force that smashes things flat.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals an ancient chess set on its way to a museum.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: While in Australia, Matt stumbles upon VENOM pulling a hologram trick of dancing rocks on the Aborigines who think the rocks are gods.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Home Stretch Pinky': Horse trainer Pinky tries to help Granny win the Kentucky Fedora - the world's famous horse race. 'Pink Pucks': To win a big hockey game, Pink conjures up Slusho to play goalie, only to have him lured away by an ice seductress.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Matt's friend, the Duke, needs three rings to unlock a treasure for his country, but he only has two.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted are discouraged when their fourth concert bombs and decide to give up music and the Wyld Stallions. Without telling the boys that their decision will jeopardize the future,
programme.sub-title: When The Going Gets Tough...Bill And Ted Are History

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Using a special gelatin formula, gels the water in the canals of Venice in order to dig up Cleopatra's golden barge.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Hardluck Hotel
programme.desc: Duckula goes to stay at a worn out and run down hotel for the weekend.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The time-travelling grandfather clock makes its return as it appears at Windsor Castle.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pinxillated
programme.desc: When a little boy can't win enough tickets for a prize at the video arcade, Pink Panther helps him out with his video game prowess.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Ghostly Gold
programme.desc: Duckula travels to the black mountains in the Yukon to find treasure and gold.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Ghost of McCastle McDuckula
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny, on holiday in Scotland hope to stay at the Glen Sparrows Hotel, but Igor has other ideas.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Black & White & Pink All Over:' Detective Pinky agrees to locate a pesky little girl's doll. 'Beach Blanket Pinky': The Panther must rally the regulars at his favorite beach to defeat the Dogfather and company who have marked the beach as theirs.
programme.sub-title: Black & White & Pink All Over/Beach Blanket Pinky

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Pilgrim Panther': Puritan Chef Pinky competes with an Indian Brave to capture a wild turkey. 'The Old Pink Magic': While performing magic at a party, Pinky accidentally makes the birthday boy disappear and has to rescue him from a wacky witch.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Dino Sour Head': An inventive prehistoric Pink Panther must help rid his village of a pesky Tyrannosaur who is causing trouble. 'The Luck o' The Pinkish': While hunting for a four-leaf clover, Pink runs afoul of a mischievous Leprechaun.
programme.sub-title: Dino Sour Head/The Luck O' The Pinkish

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: A map is pieced together from drawings on the backs of paintings in the Louvre which shows where the remote control is to explode the bombs which were placed in Paris during the German occupation.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are chosen as ambassadors to meet space aliens.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are on holiday in Spain. The delusional Don Coyote kidnaps Penfold and forces him to act as his assistant, 'Sancho Penfold'.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Show Must Go On
programme.desc: Duckula decides to become an actor and stages the story of Jack and the Magic Walnut at the castle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold takes a long time to have his passport photos taken at a booth, but he gets a sheet that says "You have broken the lens.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted find themselves with a problem: how to get part-time jobs when they have no experience. They manage to get references from Marie Antoinette and Lucretia Borgia but decide that working on music is preferable to working at a real job.
programme.sub-title: A Job, A Job, My Kingdom For A Job

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: When Ted's guitar is destroyed, the guys decide to replace it with a Fender Stratocaster that Mr. Preston had when Bill was a baby. Bill and Ted encounter themselves as toddlers and end up in a babysitting nightmare.
programme.sub-title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventure In Baby

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Astro Pink
programme.desc: Pink Panther blasts off on an adventure to save the Earth from being part of super-villain Big Nose's planet collection.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted visit Badger Cadger in a 1950's sitcom and have a run-in with Jeddi Raskell.
programme.sub-title: Leave It To Bill And Ted

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to the North Pole on news that Baron Greenback and Stiletto have been selling over a thousand heat lamps to the Eskimos and causing the polar ice caps to melt.
programme.sub-title: I Spy with My Little Eye...

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold types up his memoirs. Colonel K tells them that things around the city are suddenly vanishing with a loud "kazonk". Then Danger Mouse and Penfold are kazonked into a void-like dimension by Dr. Crumhorn.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to get Danger Mouse to bring him four ingredients needed for an evil spell to take over the world: The hair of a yeti, straw from a witch's broom, a piece of the London fog monster, and the tail feathers of Count Duckula.
programme.sub-title: The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Muff The Magic Dragon': The Panther works as a wizard's assistant in a medieval castle trying to cure the princess' allergies. 'Pink Thumb': The Panther and the voodoo man work together at an engineering company on the perfect 'garbage disposal.'

programme.title: Count Duckula: Restoration Comedy
programme.desc: Bored with the look of the castle, Duckula hires a designer to redecorate the castle.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted's adventures take them to the North Pole with Admiral Byrd, ancient Egypt, and King Tut, and finally to Dr. Livingston in the African jungles as they search for a rare exotic bird.
programme.sub-title: Birds Of A Feather Stick To The Roof Of Your Mouth

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback develops age-altering bombs which turn Danger Mouse into an over eager youth and puts Penfold on the verge of senility.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted return to Medieval times in England so they can bring the Princess babes back to sing with the Wyld Stallions at the school dance.
programme.sub-title: A Black Knight In San Dimas

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals an ancient stone tablet that has hieroglyphic clues to the Tomb of Tutiphon which contains a golden scarab with the secret of life.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Mayhem steals the Book of Power from some monks hoping it will lead him to an ancient city full of riches.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 2
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a flat in south London that could benefit from more than just cosmetic surgery, a house in a prime location in Herne Bay and a mid terrace near a main road in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a property in Maida Vale, a former stable in Wiltshire and two bed semi in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Warrington, Dorset and Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 12
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a flat in London, a house in Kent and a house in Greater Manchester.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 4
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a cluttered property in Derbyshire, a semi detached property in Kent and a three bedroomed terraced house in South Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a restaurant in Kent, a semi in Stoke and a maisonette in Uxbridge.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view a detached house in Devon, a property in Kent and a flat in Brighton.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 30
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin travel to Wiltshire to see an incredible Grade II star listed property going under the hammer! Then, it's onto Battersea, London to see a one bedroom garden flat, and in coastal Plymouth, Devon there's an unusual cottage up for auction.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 16
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a place with a low guide price in Derby, a three bed semi-detached house in Kent and a property in Stratford, east London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander look at properties in Canterbury, St. Helens and Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 15
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a three bedroomed terraced house near Wigan, a property in Sheerness and a property in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit houses Burnley, Kent and North London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 17
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in Derby in need of a new layout; a Berkshire bungalow which is perfect if you're peckish for a property; and finally, a fire damaged Cornish semi in the village of St Blazey.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 8
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a property in Tavistock in Devon, a flat in Wembley and a semi detached property in Swindon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin see an end of terrace in Ilkeston near Derby; a plot of land with planning consent in industrial Leyton in East London; and in the Wiltshire village of Atworth a tiny cottage with low ceilings, a wild garden and plenty of little secrets.

programme.title: The Hunchback Of Notre Dame
programme.desc: In 15th-century Paris, the brother of the archdeacon plots with the gypsy king to foment a peasant revolt. Meanwhile, a freakish hunchback falls in love with the gypsy queen.

programme.title: The Bees
programme.desc: When South American killer bees, corporately smuggled into the United States, mutates into intelligent insects and attacks helpless people, young scientist work desperately to end the threat as the menace swarms in on the city areas.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Bogus Journey
programme.desc: In this sequel to BILL & TED'S EXCELLENT ADVENTURE, the dimwitted duo travel to Heaven, Hell and points in between as they meet (among others) Albert Einstein, the Easter Bunny and the Grim Reaper.

programme.title: Pennywise: The Story Of IT
programme.desc: Exploring the 1990 mini-series, based on Stephen King's iconic novel IT, this documentary tells a story heard by few.

programme.title: Diary of a Nudist
programme.desc: A reporter infiltrates a nudist camp for a story but finds herself embracing the lifestyle, challenging her own beliefs and societal expectations.

programme.title: The Frog Prince
programme.desc: A young British girl journeys to Paris to go to college, and is determined to find the man of her dreams and fall madly in love. However, things don't work out quite the way she planned.

programme.title: 12 Angry Men
programme.desc: Eleven jurors are convinced that the defendant is guilty of murder. The twelfth has no doubt of his innocence. How can this one man steer the others toward the same conclusion? Henry Fonda and Lee J. Cobb lead the all-star cast in this classic!

programme.title: Clambake
programme.desc: The king of rock 'n' roll, Elvis Presley, is as a lonely million-heir who gives up his fortune and trades identities with a water ski instructor to learn about life from the bottom up in this musical romp! Bill Bixby and Shelley Fabares also star.

programme.title: Run Silent, Run Deep
programme.desc: The legendary Clark Gable and Burt Lancaster star in this gripping, classic military drama as a submarine commander (Gable) drives his men and his 1st officer (Lancaster) to the brink of mutiny in his quest to sink an enemy sub. Robert Wise directs.

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: A man in London tries to help a counter-espionage Agent. But when the Agent is killed, and the man stands accused, he must go on the run to save himself and stop a spy ring that is trying to steal top-secret information.

programme.title: Euronews
programme.desc: Euronews offers a unique perspective on world events. It empowers people to make up their own mind by delivering factual analysis and a diversity of viewpoints. Because all views matter, Euronews is "All Views".

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: In some cases, the transformation from everyday woman to brutal killer is activated by a man. A boyfriend or husband can become the focus of a deadly obsession.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women burn madly with jealousy, an anger so deep they’ll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn from “green with envy” to “lethal with jealousy?" Find out as we explore their hearts of darkness.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Under His Control
programme.desc: These are love stories with bleak, bloody endings. Blinded by desire, obsessed and malleable, some women fall into lethal relationships with dangerous, controlling men.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women are more often prey than predators, but the exceptions are deadly women indeed. In this episode, we’ll discover what drove these deadly women to prey on the unsuspecting.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Greed is one of the oldest motives for murder, and these deadly women are no exception: they’ll do anything to satisfy their need for cash. This is the dangerous world of the fortune hunters.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Crimes of revenge are usually single acts of passion. A brew of hatred and restitution boils over in a manic moment of fury. Once caught, revenge killers usually display remorse and atonement.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While their sisters chose a life of sensible, suburban motherhood, these deadly women chose a life of crime. They lived with the thrills, the danger and the deadly consequences of being outlaws.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married to Murder
programme.desc: To the outside world, their marriages seemed happy. But behind closed doors, there was no domestic bliss. These women wanted out, but getting a divorce wasn’t their preferred option.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed. From husbands to strangers to other people’s babies, they spilt blood for money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The mind of a female killer is shaped by a complex cocktail of influences. Do people become that way, driven by horrendous events in their lives? Or are some killers born bad?

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love to Death
programme.desc: Love and money are a beautiful marriage, for the lucky ones they create a life of happiness and ease. Unless the love is of money, the bride is a killer, and the groom their victim.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Paradise
programme.desc: It should have been Shangri-La, but when bad blood developed, piracy took over and the only law was the law of the jungle.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dixie Mafia
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a Judge and his politician wife are murdered in their home. The killer left few clues. It looked like a professional hit, and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI refused to give up.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Threat
programme.desc: A suspected drug dealer would stop at nothing to keep local and federal authorities from building a case against him. When his organization started targeting the government itself, federal agencies went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Without Remorse
programme.desc: A small town without any crime was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. Some killers are cold and calculating; others are impulsive, unpredictable, and hard to pin down.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dishonored
programme.desc: Shirley Russell was determined enough to reach the rank of Captain in the United States Marine Corps. When this officer turned up missing, her colleagues refused to believe she had gone AWOL. It was unclear whether Captain Shirley Russell was alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Killing Zone
programme.desc: In the 1990's the war on drugs was in full swing. The front lines were often America's inner cities where gangs use violence and murder to rule their empires.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Crime Spree
programme.desc: They swept through six states like a plague, thrill killers acting without rhyme or reason. They targeted adults and young girls, kidnapping, and murdering. And in their rampage, they left plenty of clues, but moving targets are the hardest to hit.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The World Trade Center Bombing
programme.desc: Terrorist bombings are what happen in other countries. Not here in the United States. And yet, in 1993, it happened in New York City.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Human Prey
programme.desc: Thomas Dillon killed five outdoorsmen at random over a period of three years in rural southern Ohio. After a tip led the FBI to the murder weapon, ballistic testing made a match.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The C-11 Squad
programme.desc: New York City is not stranger to gang violence. But when a 12-year-old child was kidnapped, the gangs had crossed a line. Law enforcement raced to find the missing boy before it was too late.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A daring kidnapping and a bizarre ransom note baffled Mississippi authorities. The victim was the wife of a wealthy businessman. A list of 12 names and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Business
programme.desc: Many escape the poverty of India to start new lives in the United States. One turned the American dream into a nightmare. People started turning up dead in New York's Indian community-authorities struggled to link fraud and murder to a powerful man.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Murdering Cowboy
programme.desc: The sun set on the Wild West long ago, but some people refused to notice. Claude Dallas was one of them. A wily trapper, Dallas typified the rugged individualism that forged this country. He went too far when he killed two game wardens in 1981.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Predator
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dead Run
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania, an inmate escaped with the help of his female accomplice. Following a trail of fraudulent credit card receipts and deaths, authorities track the pair, but always find themselves one step behind.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Lord Chitterly, an English traitor who looks like the bumbling Colonel Crittendon, enters Stalag 13 with information for the Germans. Crittendon is made up to replace the Lord and fool the Germans.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Lord Chitterly, an English traitor who looks like the bumbling Colonel Crittendon, enters Stalag 13 with information for the Germans. Crittendon is made up to replace the Lord and fool the Germans. The real Lord escapes.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The heroes try to prevent the Gestapo from sending Klink and Schultz to the Russian front.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Schultz is now the temporary Kommandant of Stalag 13 and starts to abuse his new power.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan and company tell a German mole, disguised as an American aviator, all about their operation.
programme.sub-title: Eight O'Clock And All Is Well

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.desc: Was Presidential hopeful, Robert F. Kennedy really killed by disgruntled Palestinian Sirhan Sirhan as we’ve been led to believe? Or was he another in a long line of Manchurian candidates, a victim of a long standing mind control program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: What on Earth are they spraying? Richard travels into the field to meet several researchers who present compelling evidence that chemicals or biological agents are being deliberately sprayed into the atmosphere by some shadowy government agency.

programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.desc: Aiken, South Carolina, is a tiny peaceful town where nothing much happens. Until Jessica Carpenter, 17, is found raped and murdered in her living room. The neighborhood goes on high alert while police swing into action to catch her killer.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Jessica Carpenter
programme.desc: Aiken, South Carolina, is a tiny peaceful town where nothing much happens. Until Jessica Carpenter, 17, is found raped and murdered in her living room. The neighborhood goes on high alert while police swing into action to catch her killer.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Stomped to Death
programme.desc: Just outside Memphis, an affluent Tennessee community is stunned when, in the early morning hours, a gentle, much-loved McDonalds manager is brutally attacked and left for dead on the floor of the restaurant.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Who Killed Daddy’s Little Girl?

programme.title: Cold Blood: The Strangler
programme.desc: Cheryl Miller is a beautiful and popular young college student in Saginaw, Michigan. But on June 15th 1974, she is brutally raped, strangled and left for dead on her bedroom floor.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Hollywood Homicide
programme.desc: An aspiring society photographer from Switzerland is brutally murdered in his upscale Los Angeles home. And when officers investigate, they find a scene worthy of a horror film. Blood has dripped from the ceiling and down walls.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mortal Passion

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mr. Big

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Hell's Gate

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Incorrible

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Short Fuse
programme.desc: Explosives Investigations: The crime lab is the place where science meets murder. In New York State, Eleanor Fowler opened a small package, which as mailed to her home. When she lifted the lid the box exploded killing her instantly.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Murder For Hire
programme.desc: For some killers, murder can be a profitable business. And the scene of the crime can be both a source for clues, and puzzling questions. When a victim has been targeted for death, investigators must look beyond the obvious to uncover a murder for hire.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Tainted Blood
programme.desc: The forces of nature can reduce a body to bones in a matter of weeks. Using a unique combination of art and science, forensic anthropologists give victims a face long after they have been forgotten.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Remnant of Blame
programme.desc: The solution to the most heinous crimes often hinge on the smallest of clues. Investigators must have their eyes trained to find the full story of a murder written in a single scrap of evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly intentions
programme.desc: When abduction turns to murder, forensic science is the only key to finding justice for the victims of a kidnapper's deadly intentions.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Random Targets
programme.desc: Most victims of multiple murderers are meticulously chosen because of a mutual connection with the killer or because they match an intricate set of criteria that fits the killer's MO.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Stranger Than Fiction
programme.desc: A millionaire is found dead, murdered for a stash of buried silver. A young woman dates violent men, only to be killed by her best friend.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Burning Evidence
programme.desc: Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Unlikely Sources
programme.desc: Some of the best clues come from the least likely places. Baffling crimes have been solved and criminals betrayed through evidence provided by insects, beer bottles, and other seemingly meaningless objects.

programme.title: The New Detectives: A Deadly Smile
programme.desc: Investigators rely on forensic odontology to identify a body from a single tooth and to catch two brutal killers from their bite marks.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Out To Kill
programme.desc: An abandoned car outside Philadelphia brings heartbreak to a family and terror to a community. A young woman is dead, the killer gone. But the marks of his passage remain.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Fatal Abduction
programme.desc: Anytime, anywhere, people disappear, kidnapped from their daily routines. Predators always leave clues behind, but chasing them takes time, hampering investigators' attempts to solve these fatal abductions.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Loved To Death
programme.desc: A victim usually never foresees danger when the perpetrator turns out to be a friend or a lover. Science and microscopic evidence can unmask these killers and find justice for those who are Loved To Death.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Shattered Vows
programme.desc: A woman is found dead at the bottom of the basement stairs. As detectives look into the accident, they begin to question the sequence of events.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Three hairs microscopic fibers a common trash bag ripped from a roll. Seemingly small and insignificant clues become a victim's silent witness.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Without A Trace
programme.desc: Missing Person: Approximately 1.8 million Americans are reported missing each year. Worldwide, the number of missing persons nearly triples.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Trial Of The Century
programme.desc: Forensics in the O.J. Simpson Murder Trial: Millions watched on television as the jury rendered their verdict. Orenthal James Simpson was found not guilty of murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy
programme.desc: Forensic Sculpting: Forensic sculptors retrieve people from oblivion. Using clay and an intricate knowledge of anatomy, forensic arts place a face on an unidentified skull, recreating the victim's likeness, which often leads to his name.

programme.title: Andromeda: Cui Bono
