programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Jean says her 17-year-old daughter, Cheyenne, was the all-American girl , a cheerleader, track star and straight-A honor student until she began dating Josh, a 28-year-old divorced father. Dr. Phil takes a look at Josh's history, should Jean be concerned?
programme.sub-title: He's Nearly 30 And Engaged To My Teen With Braces

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: When you imagine getting an inheritance, you might assume all your financial problems would be solved. But can the money be more trouble than it's worth?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Are you the kind of neighbor who loans a cup of sugar and always waves hello, or do you keep to yourself and call the cops the minute you hear loud music? Dr. Phil talks to guests who say they definitely don't live in Mr. Roger's neighborhood!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Dr. Phil takes an intense look at the life of Star, a stay-at-home mom who fears that her dangerous habit may take a turn for the worse. Star's 11-year-old daughter, Autumn, sends Dr. Phil a poignant plea for help before tragedy strikes.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: In a bizarre abuse case, Gypsy Rose Blanchard's mother forced her to pretend she was ill, undergo countless surgeries, shave her head, insert a feeding tube & use a wheelchair. A web of deceit & torture led to murder. Gypsy Rose talks with Dr. Phil.Pt. 1
programme.sub-title: Mother Knows Best: The Story Of Gypsy Rose Pt.1

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Dr. Phil continues his conversation with Trisha, a mother and wife, who is live-streaming her entire life. Find out what caused Trisha to storm off and collapse on stage. Did Dr. Phil almost have to call the paramedics? Pt. 2
programme.sub-title: YouTube Obsessed Trisha's Dramatic Collapse On Stage Pt.2

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Do you have a relative scheming to get money from a rich aunt, uncle or even parents when they pass away?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: If you dread Valentine's Day and don't have a creative bone in your body, take heart, because Dr. Phil and Robin are here to help the romantically challenged!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Tom is 100% convinced his fiancée, Teaka, is cheating on him with his friends, neighbors, coworkers, & even the mailman! Tom has all-new video evidence to show Dr. Phil & Teaka to prove Teaka's unfaithfulness. Teaka declares she has never cheated on Tom.
programme.sub-title: Is My Fiance Cheating With The Mailman?!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Wife Nicole thought she knew her husband of 20 years, Carter, but all that changed as she uncovered a hidden secret. Nicole discovered Carter has been having a 4-year sexual affair with a man. Find out the shocking results a private investigator reveals.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Donna has extreme hatred for her daughter-in-law, Jen, and it's coming between her relationship with her son, Brad. Jen & Brad say they're so terrified of Donna, they changed the locks and sleep with a gun. Can Dr. Phil calm these feuding in-laws?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Baylen bravely posted TikTok videos of her daily struggles with Tourette syndrome and now has over 3 million followers. Singer/Influencer Lele Pons says she also suffers from OCD, Tourette syndrome, & depression. They discuss their experiences.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Jason Collier was a perfect boyfriend, handsome, & a well-respected police chief of a Texas town. But multiple women he was dating discovered that Jason Collier was every woman's worst nightmare: a married man lying to his wife but also to them. Pt. 2
programme.sub-title: Texas Police Chief Leading Secret Triple Life Pt.2

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Are you a 'social media lurker?' How often do you scroll through social media platforms to stare at other people's photos and videos and decide that person is surely living their best life, and you are not. A deep dive into posting: real vs fake.
programme.sub-title: Social Media: The Real Vs. The Façade

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Jennifer believes she & Russian Pres. Vladimir Putin are in love; the Lord has revealed she will be Putin's wife, the Mother of Russia, & birth him a child. Family syas Jen feels she's intercepted Trump's assassination attemp, terrorist plots, and 4 wars.
programme.sub-title: My Mom Is Homeless And Believes She Is Engaged To Putin

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Brad says he can't choose between his wife, Michele, and his girlfriend, who's calling herself "Sarah" to protect her identity. Pt.1

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: In 2011, Ashlee's husband, Emmett, was having an affair with co-worker Kandi, when he was shot & killed by Kandi's husband, Rob. The story rocked their small Idaho town & left two families forever changed. Ashlee & Kandi come face- to-face on stage.
programme.sub-title: Deadly Affair: The Widow, The Lover, & A Spouse Behind Bars

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Karen writes to Dr. Phil saying her husband mentally abuses her by telling her how fat she is, how terrible she looks, criticizes her makeup, calls her names, tells fat jokes. She hides in a bathroom to eat and he won't touch her "until she loses 200 lbs"

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Natalie, 22, says her boyfriend Justin, 25, has an anger problem, and she fears for her life and for their 10-month-old son. Both admit their child has witnessed turmoil. Does this young relationship have a future?
programme.sub-title: Young, In Love And In Over Their Heads

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Pam says her ex-husband, Ed, is enabling, Andee's, 24, addiction by giving her money, which she uses for drugs. Ed admits he allows Andee to manipulate him but says he's afraid she'll resort to prostitution or worse. Can Ed learn to change his approach?
programme.sub-title: My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Daughter

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Victoria wrote to Dr. Phil after she says her daughter, Jessica, 18, physically attacked her on multiple occasions, leaving a gash in her head from a cellphone & her house damaged, as well as her body bruised after a Thanksgiving day family throw down.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny gets hired to do a full bumper-to-bumper restoration on a '66 Mustang Convertible. Unfortunately, when the car is stripped down, they find there's not a whole lot of car between her bumpers.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny and the team continue wrestling with the stock restoration Mustang mess, hoping for some extra funds and a lot of patience from their client. Meanwhile, Kevin spots a rare old Grand Prix.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny and the team are commissioned to restore a 1941 Triumph motorcycle back to its WWII era glory. Even with a $50,000 budget, it turns into one of the biggest challenges in Count's Kustoms history.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny continues working on a WWII era Triumph motorcycle for a client hoping to have it as a tribute to his family's military roots. When they get it all together and try to crank it, it won't start.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny builds a world-class bike for a high end client, and if all goes well it could mean a ton of new business for the shop. Meanwhile, Mike and Ryan are on the hunt for the perfect Mustang.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: A high end client has his heart set on a tricked out '68 Camaro, so Danny sets out to find one and give it the world class Counts Kustoms treatment. Kevin spots a 1969 AMX that Danny has always wanted.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Ryan reluctantly goes down the Caddy customization wormhole on a personal project, and everyone at the shop is happy to watch him go, just to see how great it turns out.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Shannon recruits the guys to help him finish up a gift for Danny - a bike he started building for him ten years ago. Danny spots a 1969 Corvette in a friend's lot and he can't resist paying a visit.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny charges Ryan with customizing a new Chevy Silverado for their most difficult customer--himself! Danny wants it to maintain the flavor of his old '56 truck, but wants more comfort and reliability.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: The team is commissioned to build a bike for a military veteran. He wants to show his love for family and country by covering the bike with American flags and portraits of loved ones who also served.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: When Tommy Lee's beloved bike gets crushed in an accident, he turns to the only person he trusts to fix it--the guys who built it--Count's Kustoms.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Ryan, Mike and Shannon convince Danny to sponsor them in a 24-hour junk car endurance race. Meanwhile Danny has the guys work on a one of a kind special shop project as payback for his sponsorship.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: The guys finally find a not so perfect car for the junk car race which they resurrect as the "Count's Bloody Mess". At the race, they realize first place may not be an option, but hope that "Count's Bloody Mess" will finish in one piece.
programme.sub-title: The Fast and the Ridiculous, Part 2

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Twisted Sister's Dee Snider brings the crew a brand new BMW Z4 that he wants Count-ified for his wife Suzette as an anniversary present. A simple paint job turns disaster when Suzette is not happy with the final look, not once, but twice.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Kevin's brother Steve wants a 1980 Kawasaki KZ100 motorcycle tricked out just like the ones on the classic TV series, CHiPs.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Ryan and Kevin find a 1972 Datsun 240z that Danny agrees to flip. But when he has a last-minute change of heart, Ryan must figure out how to make Danny's vision a reality without derailing the project.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny and Shannon are in motorcycle heaven when they are asked to restore a rare 1957 Harley Davidson Pan head prototype that has spent the last 20 years in their client's living room.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: The local Fox News channel wants to update their Ford Expedition Storm Chaser in a mobile news unit with an elaborate paint scheme that they can also bring to schools to help kids learn about weather.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: After the vehicle that holds a land speed record is seriously damaged and repaired, the guys give it a sleek look. Then, the crew heads to Bonneville, UT to watch the Turbinator go for the record again.

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 3
programme.desc: Tiff Needell conducts a shocking crash test to show why everyone should make sure their next car has electronic stability control. Vicki Butler-Henderson is at an exclusive motor show in Monte Carlo, where she reveals the latest Pagani hypercar.

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 4
programme.desc: Tiff Needell enters the opening round of the exclusive Lamborghini Super Trofeo race series – with advice from the man with every petrolhead's dream job – Lamborghini chief test driver Giorgio Sanna.

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 5
programme.desc: Jason Plato is in Iceland being taught how to race a brutal Formula Off-Road buggy up a cliff of volcanic rock by a former champion. Tiff Needell is joined by former Le Mans winner Guy Smith to test a brand new version of Bentley's best selling car

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 6
programme.desc: Jason Plato is joined by ex-Ferrari F1 driver Mika Salon to pit the brand new £100,000 Mercedes C63 AMG Black Series against its key rival from BMW

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 7
programme.desc: Jonny Smith goes behind the scenes at the gruelling Isle of Man TT motorbike race, and spends the day with TT hero and 19 times winner John McGuinness. Tiff Needell races around the infamous TT circuit in the brand new Subaru BRZ sports car.

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 8
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson is in Denmark experiencing the exciting world of motorbike speedway with triple world champion Nicki Pederson. Jonny Smith visits Ferrari's hi-tech aluminium factory before taking their latest California supercar out

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 9
programme.desc: Jonny Smith is at the biggest American car show in the world – which takes place just outside a small town in Sweden. He judges the prestigious best in show competition, goes on a late night cruise and tests the brand new Corvette Grand Sport.

programme.title: Fifth Gear: Episode 10
programme.desc: Vicki holds a spectacular race against a jet plane, in the ultimate test of the brand new Porsche Boxster. Jonny heads to Norway to test the all new Ford Focus ST hot hatch on an incredible mountain road that every driver should visit in their lifetime

programme.desc: Watch adrenaline-fueled car chases and high-speed drama, including live coverage of real-time chases.

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Maddox is home but Talia's struggling post C-Section. Sophie invests in her own beauty business. Marnie's dream house move might be back on and Government travel restrictions impact Holly. (S4, ep9)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Aaron misses his train to a press conference announcing his big fight, will he make it in time? Holly gets a tattoo removed and Marnie has her baby scan. Gary invites Aaron to meet Chester. (S1, ep1)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: With the house reduced to carnage and Sophie losing even more faith in boyfriend Joel, there are still plenty of beaches to party on. Meanwhile Holly ups her game in the pursuit of James. (S3, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: Sophie is elated as Joel surprises her with a visit. But will he be able to behave himself? And with everyone fearing that his worldie mission may now be over, Holly moves in on her prey. (S3, ep3)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: The chaos in Cancun continues as Gaz celebrates his birthday in true Geordie style. Charlotte takes her holiday romance to the next level. Vicky erupts as Cancun Chris gets sweet revenge. (S3, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: Vicky and Ricci return to the house in time for Becca's birthday which goes off in usual Geordie style. Sophie sends Joel back to England, but it's not long before she regrets the decision. (S3, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: An unexpected call means James has to choose between his best mates and Holly. Gary has another threesome and a night out trying to forget about Gary ends in disaster for Charlotte. (S3, ep6)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: The boy-girl divide returns! Charlotte and Gaz aren't speaking, Becca and Jay are continually arguing, while Holly and James' nurse-patient relationship is back to strictly professional. (S3, ep7)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: It's the final few days of spring break and the house is rocked by the loss of yet another member. In the last hours, to the disbelief of everyone, Ricci plans his proposal to Vicky. (S3, ep8)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: We believe our Geordies have gone above and beyond in services to tashing on and getting mortal for the past three series, so it’s time we reward them for services to fake tan and flirting.

programme.title: Geordie Shore: Holly's First Date
programme.desc: With Ricci and Sophie having been asked to leave the house, the rest of the Geordie Shore gang decide a road trip to Liverpool is in order to check out the clubs and talent.

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Aaron misses his train to a press conference announcing his big fight, will he make it in time? Holly gets a tattoo removed and Marnie has her baby scan. Gary invites Aaron to meet Chester. (S1, ep1)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie and Casey do a photo shoot for OK! Magazine. Holly takes a class in public speaking and it's date night for Gary and Emma. The stress of Aaron's upcoming fight is beginning to show. (S1, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie gives Casey a sexy birthday present. Holly faces her fears at Sussex Uni. Aaron meets up with Nathan and Sam to talk about mental health. Emma has a big surprise for Gary. (S1, ep3)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie shows off her baby bump in a magazine shoot. Gary plays golf with Chris Hughes. Aaron's shocked to find out who his opponent is in his MMA fight and Holly says a tearful goodbye. (S1, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Gary and Emma share some special news and Aaron gets stuck into training. Holly goes to the House of Lords and Marnie and Casey get a shock when an photo of Casey is sent to a newspaper. (S1, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie could give birth at any moment. Sophie opens up to Holly about her heartbreak. Chester gives Gary the run around and Aaron faces up to the reality of becoming a Dad. (S2, ep1)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Gary plans a life-changing surprise for Emma. Holly goes to see some unicorns. Sophie struggles to forget her ex and Marnie has a traumatic labour. (S2, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Sophie gets ready to celebrate her 30th birthday in style. Marnie adjusts to her new life. Holly takes Jacob shopping and Gary prepares to go down on one knee. (S2, ep3)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie's illness catches up with her. Gary takes Aaron "Dad training" and Sophie receives some devastating news. (S2, ep4)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: Bondi Rescue follows the work of the elite professional Lifeguards who patrol Australia's busiest beach - Bondi. Home of the weird, wonderful, beautiful, deadly and dangerous, join Australia’s favourite lifeguards on Bondi Rescue 24/7.

programme.title: JAG: Dream Team
programme.desc: A sailor dies during a fight aboard a ship at sea; Bud discovers the explanation. Mattie wakes up and talks. The general announces a reorganization, orders, and a promotion.

programme.title: JAG: Pilot, Part 1 & 2
programme.desc: A woman radar-intercept officer (RIO) disappears at night from an aircraft carrier in the Adriatic Sea. Two JAG lawyers, Lt. Harm Rabb and Lt. Caitlin Pike, investigate and solve. Harm, a former Naval aviator, unexpectedly becomes a hero.

programme.title: JAG: Shadow
programme.desc: A civilian tech rep aboard a submarine seizes control of an experimental torpedo after its launch during an exercise

programme.title: JAG: déjà vu (AKA Soldier of Love)
programme.desc: Three Navy lieutenants die by homicide in 11 months around Washington. Harm and Meg investigate

programme.title: JAG: Desert Son
programme.desc: Due to a human error in an exercise, artillery fire injures seven Marines, one of whom later dies.

programme.title: JAG: Brig Break
programme.desc: A sailor has stolen two Stinger missiles from a base. Harm and Meg investigate.

programme.title: JAG: Pilot Error
programme.desc: Two Naval aviators die in a crash of an F-14 while using a new fly-by-wire navigation system.

programme.title: JAG: War Cries
programme.desc: In Lima, Peru, while a boy sneaks into the US embassy at night, a Marine guard stops him.

programme.title: JAG: Sightings
programme.desc: In south Texas, residents have reported strange phenomena, stories of supernatural events have abounded, and a 10-year-old girl has disappeared.

programme.title: JAG: Scimitar
programme.desc: Two Marines in a Humvee mistakenly drive into Iraq; an Iraqi detail intercepts them, kills one, and captures the other.

programme.title: JAG: Defensive Action
programme.desc: A patrolling F-14 malfunctions in a no-fly zone; the crew ejects, and the bird explodes.

programme.title: JAG: Boot.
programme.desc: A woman Marine recruit dies at Parris Island. Harm investigates while Meg poses undercover as a recruit.

programme.title: JAG: The Brotherhood
programme.desc: During a landing exercise Marines find a beaten, comatose fellow Marine on the beach

programme.title: JAG: Hemlock
programme.desc: By mistake a foreign agent sends a fax to Meg's office; then he retrieves it and shoots Meg to prevent her from identifying him

programme.title: JAG: High Ground
programme.desc: A Marine sniper receives orders contrary to a previous deal; his CO refuses to listen; the sniper takes a shot at the CO's Humvee.

programme.title: JAG: Smoked
programme.desc: After a lightning strike, an F-14 wanders into Cuban airspace, and Cuban fighters force it to land

programme.title: JAG: Black Ops
programme.desc: A Naval aviator, while temporarily assigned to a SEAL team, drowns at sea; he was the son of a woman senator.

programme.title: JAG: Survivors
programme.desc: In a civilian court Harm represents a divorced (and emotionally baffled) Marine colonel, who has gotten into trouble over an unauthorized visit with his minor son.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: COPS: Cops A Man Without a Plan
programme.desc: A young man receives a wake-up call when police catch him breaking the law, and officers play peacemakers after a board game sparks a family feud.
programme.sub-title: Cops A Man Without a Plan

programme.title: COPS: Cops The Truth Is Key
programme.desc: A desperate suspect flees from the cops after shots are fired, and a father and daughter are caught off-guard when they run into a man wielding a screwdriver.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Mother Knows Best
programme.desc: A defiant motorist is shocked when his mother sides with the law, and deputies discover a man wearing a bizarre costume during a routine traffic stop.

programme.title: COPS: Cops The Naked Truth
programme.desc: An evasive driver blames his girlfriend for his behavior, a pregnant woman makes some questionable choices, and a robbery suspect finds comfort in coming clean.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Rock Star
programme.desc: Officers intervene when a dispute between neighbors gets heated, suspects of a shooting lead police on a high-speed pursuit, and a domestic argument gets out of hand.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Window of Opportunity
programme.desc: Officers apprehend an armed suspect, the cops are called when a man damages his neighbor's window, and deputies investigate a parking garage known for illegal activity.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Mommy's Tears
programme.desc: A suspect collides with another driver while fleeing from police, deputies mediate an altercation over a bag of chips, and officers respond to a domestic dispute.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Running Scared
programme.desc: Suspected jewelry thieves attempt a daring escape during a pursuit, a man gives officers a challenge during his arrest, and a traffic stop puts a marriage in jeopardy.

programme.title: COPS: Multiple Identities
programme.desc: Officers recover a baggie of drugs from a crying suspect. A drunk woman hides a second ID in her back pocket. Police stop a vehicle that hit a patrol car.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Four Felonies and a Flat Tire
programme.desc: A suspected car thief's claims of innocence are undermined by a video he shot of himself earlier that day, and the pursuit of a suspected drunk driver has a surprising end.
programme.sub-title: Cops Four Felonies and a Flat Tire

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Police go off-roading to follow a stolen car, a man has odd excuses for having suspicious items, and an officer settles a dispute between a wife and her jealous husband.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Cookies and Contraband
programme.desc: A detective is injured after chasing a man driving a stolen car, a bar fight ends with a couple in handcuffs, and an officer finds a variety of items during a vehicle search.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A disgruntled man is tased, leaving his unhoused wife to fend for herself. A sovereign citizen is arrested for a stolen firearm. A man in possession of meth claims a bag belongs to his girlfriend.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Undeniably Fishy
programme.desc: A man loses his friend and finds trouble, officers stop a vehicle they believe was involved in a shooting, and a suspicious vehicle leads to a suspicious story.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Highway to Jail
programme.desc: Reckless ATV drivers lead police on a dangerous chase through the streets of Atlanta, and a man worried about warrants lies about his identity to police during a traffic stop.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: After leading police on a slow pursuit, a sharply-dressed driver has a heated talk with another deputy. In spite of her tears, police unravel a woman’s lies one-by-one. A traditionally bad omen turns into good luck for a couple.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dream Date
programme.desc: Something’s cooking when Aunts Hilda and Zelda use man dough to bake a date for Sabrina.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: 3rd Aunt From The Sun
programme.desc: As fun Aunt Vesta, Raquel Welch shows Sabrina how the Other Realm half live.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Magic Joel
programme.desc: Sabrina’s job as a magician’s assistant is short-lived when she accidentally makes the boss disappear.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Geek Like Me
programme.desc: Popular Libby manages to turn being a geek into a position of power.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sweet N’ Sour Victory
programme.desc: Sabrina casts a spell on herself to become an instant Kung-Fu master.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: A Girl & Her Cat
programme.desc: A Christmas Eve catnapping renews Salem’s love for his family.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Trial By Fury
programme.desc: Sabrina and her aunts use magic to show an unfair teacher that what goes around comes around.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Jenny’s Non-Dream
programme.desc: Behaving as mortals for just one night turns out to be more than the Spellman household can handle.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Through The Looking Glass
programme.desc: Sabrina's in a foul mood and only baseball great Brady Anderson can help her out of her slump.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Hilda And Zelda: The Teenage Years
programme.desc: Aunts Hilda and Zelda experience growing pains when they turn into teenagers for the night.
programme.sub-title: Hilda And Zelda: The Teenage Years

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Mars Attracts
programme.desc: A winter vacation to Mars and a cute ski instructor test Sabrina’s feelings for Harvey.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: First Kiss
programme.desc: Sabrina’s first kiss becomes a test of true love when Harvey is turned into a frog.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sweet Charity
programme.desc: When Sabrina uses Libby’s likeness to promote a community service program, the real Libby manages to steal the limelight.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Cat Showdown
programme.desc: Salem enters a cat show to make some fast cash, but Sabrina uncovers a fur-raising scandal.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Meeting Dad’s Girlfriend
programme.desc: Love definitely isn’t in the air when Sabrina meets her dad’s new girlfriend.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: As Westbridge Turns
programme.desc: Sinister plots, tumultuous romance, danger, and intrigue – just another day at Westbridge High, after Sabrina casts a spell wishing for more excitement.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Great Mistake
programme.desc: Sabrina finds herself in a little trouble when she sneaks out of the house after being grounded.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Crucible
programme.desc: Sabrina is put on trial for witchcraft when her class visits historical Salem, Massachusetts.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Troll Bride
programme.desc: When Sabrina is tricked into marrying a troll, only a prince – a.k.a. Harvey – can save her.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Gets Her License (Part 1)
programme.desc: Sabrina's 17th birthday turns stressful when she has to start studying for her witch's license.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Gets Her License (Part 2)
programme.desc: Sabrina learns about the responsibilities that come with using magic when she earns her witch’s learner’s permit.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dummy For Love
programme.desc: Sabrina takes advantage of Vice Principal Kraft’s attraction to Aunt Hilda.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dante’s Inferno
programme.desc: Sabrina learns not to date outside of her realm after a bad evening with a witch boy.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: A Doll’s Story
programme.desc: Babysitting becomes a test of ingenuity when Sabrina’s bratty little cousin turns her into a doll.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina The Boy
programme.desc: The mysteries of men are revealed when Sabrina poses as a boy to figure out how they think.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: A River Of Candy Corn Runs Through It
programme.desc: Halloween is a scary night indeed when Sabrina and her aunts try to throw a normal party
programme.sub-title: A River Of Candy Corn Runs Through It

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Inna Gadda Sabrina
programme.desc: A time ball sends the town of Westbridge grooving back to the 1960s.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Witch Trash
programme.desc: A family feud erupts when hillbilly cousins (Loni Anderson and Alice Ghostley) arrive from the Other Realm.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: To Tell A Mortal
programme.desc: It’s Friday the 13th, and for one day only, Sabrina can tell her friends she’s a witch.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Oh What A Tangled Spell She Weaves
programme.desc: Sabrina’s lack of attention with detail causes bad side effects to her magic.
programme.sub-title: Oh What A Tangled Spell She Weaves

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Claus
programme.desc: A special visitor from the Other Realm helps Sabrina recapture the true spirit of Christmas

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Little Big Kraft
programme.desc: Tired of Mr. Kraft’s harsh rules, Sabrina concocts a potion to make him act like a teenager.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: 5 Easy Pieces Of Libby
programme.desc: Libby is even harder to figure out after Sabrina accidentally turns her into a jigsaw puzzle.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Finger Lickin’ Flu
programme.desc: Sabrina catches the flu in her pointing finger and accidentally transfers her magic to a teacher.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina & The Beanstalk
programme.desc: Sabrina’s beanstalk is right out of a fairytale, but it’s more like a nightmare for Harvey

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Equalizer
programme.desc: Roland the troll takes Valentine’s Day too far when he steals Sabrina’s heart.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Band Show
programme.desc: Egos soar out of control when Sabrina uses magic to become a rock star.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: My Nightmare The Car
programme.desc: A talking car causes Sabrina a big headache.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Quiz Show
programme.desc: Sabrina realizes the importance of her Quizmaster after she inadvertently gets him fired.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Jon accidentally orders a live Turkey instead of a cooked turkey for dinner with Liz. Garfield disguises himself as a turkey to get rid of the turkey.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield and Odie watch the news about a villain named Silent Jack, who robs houses while the owners are away.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Nermal meets a cat called Agent X who claims to be a Secret Agent, but he is really a runaway cat trying to impress the crowd.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield becomes the victim of a case of mistaken identity when he dresses up as Catzilla for Halloween.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield and Odie get their bodies swapped by a Saturn alien.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: When Nermal's owner leaves town, Nermal stays with Garfield and fakes a leg injury. Garfield and Odie plan to get rid of him.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield's teddy bear goes missing.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Odie becomes a were-dog in the middle of the night and eats lasagna. But Jon thinks that Garfield ate it.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzly imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.
programme.sub-title: The Bear That Laid Golden Eggs

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: At the Ranger's home, Grizzy finds a high-tech gadget that enables you to learn anything within seconds thanks to electrodes hooked to the brain, meaning that he can become the perfect bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: An old metal box found by chance in a recess of a cliff brings back childhood memories for Grizzy and the Lemmings.
programme.sub-title: As Far as Bear Can Remember

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to watch a documentary on salmon fish, nice and comfy on the sofa, as the Lemmings play chemistry novices with a fun experiment box.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects in his nest perched high up in a tree. This is a case of absolute urgency.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has got it into his head to offer a butterfly to the bear he's in love with. But the Lemmings fight with him over the rare specimens he's spotted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered an addictive new game based on the astonishing bouncing properties of the ranger's new running shoes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy babysits for the she-bear he has a crush on. There's no question of Grizzy tripping up, as he has no intention of taking any risks, but he hasn't reckoned on the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Unwilling to share the food both he and the Lemmings are wild about, Grizzy locks it up inside the ranger's brand new and apparently burglar-proof safe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is impressed by the TV show of a magician who transforms a partridge into a bouquet of flowers. To get rid of the Lemmings once and for all, for they exasperate him with their incessant games, Grizzy tries the magic trick on them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered a truly wild new game, an augmented reality programme on the ranger's digital tablet. Grizzy confiscates this toy and becomes a fan of the salmon fishing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The only edible food item left in the ranger's cabin is a salmon stuck inside a block of ice. Starving, Grizzly decides to melt the ice with the blow-dryer. Only the Lemmings are already using it for their latest rocket game in the piping system.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzly's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzly changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mâché statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and they start to obey it.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly discovers the leftovers of a Halloween party in the ranger's cabin, which was entirely redecorated for the occasion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When the last jar of chocolate spread they were fighting over sinks to the bottom of the river, Grizzy and the Lemmings have no choice but to dive in to recover it. But to their great surprise, they discover you can't breathe in water.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a strange figurine with the power to switch the spirits of two living beings and finds himself imprisoned in the body of a caribou.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: While salmon fishing on the riverbank, Grizzly hauls in an unusual catch: an old hand mirror straight out of a fairytale; instead of showing the reflection of the person who holds it, the mirror shows an idealized vision of his surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: In a casing that fell from a circus caravan, the Lemmings find a magic stick that can enchant objects and make them come alive.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly calls upon his fishing instincts to seduce the she-bear, but only succeeds in making a fool of himself. When he sees the Lemmings riding a magnificent wild salmon like rodeo cowboys, he tries to steal theirs.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Nutty Hill is suffering from a stifling heat wave. Searching in vain for a bit of cool air, Grizzy suddenly has a brilliant idea.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Just as Grizzly was about to coat a nice big fresh salmon with chocolate spread and snuggle up in front of the TV, he discovers that the pantry is empty.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's nap is interrupted by an annoying vibration, the forest ranger's cell phone. It has various functions, including a game to which he instantly becomes addicted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Freed by the thawing ice, a mysterious giant egg slides up to the front door of the log cabin and the Lemmings watch with interest as it hatches.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy decides to slim down with the ranger's bodybuilding equipment, but before starting his exercise routine, he will first have to settle the problem of the Lemmings, who are already using the machines as an amusement park.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: By chance, Grizzly discovers that the ranger's new robot vacuum cleaner also gives very pleasant back massages.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers a gadget that controls various functions in the cabin. He will finally be able to get rid of the Lemmings, without even leaving the couch.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: To make sure that the Lemmings don't snatch his chocolate spread, Grizzy turns the fridge into a safe. However, the Lemmings see Grizzy open the door while sleepwalking.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have invented a new popcorn game which consists of throwing popcorn and catching it with their mouths.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to learn to dance the mambo to please his lovely she-bear. He finds a learning kit at the ranger's home which has a dance carpet with footprints.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: After receiving a terrible blow on the head, Grizzly thinks he's a rodent and tries to get the gang to adopt him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Know What You Did In Drama Class
programme.desc: Diavian and Tyreke become partners in drama class but Tia flips out after catching them in a passionate kiss during a drama rehearsal. Tamera and Jordan try to hook up cable in the girl's room
programme.sub-title: I Know What You Did In Drama Class

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Talk
programme.desc: Jordan's guest shot on Tamera's radio show turns into a full-time gig, but it may cost them their friendship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Freaknik
programme.desc: Tia, Tamera, and Diavian all go to FreakNik in Atlanta during Spring Break. Tyreke races to Atlanta hoping to make up with Tia, but it may not be in time to save their relationship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Before There Was Hip-Hop
programme.desc: An old-time jazz legend fuels dreams of the swing era for the class and dreams of heroics for Jordan.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Let Them Eat Cupcake
programme.desc: A series of mix-ups delays Victor's marriage proposal to Lisa

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Road Less Traveled
programme.desc: Tia considers taking a summer internship with the WNBA in New York. Meanwhile, Lisa worries about her upcoming wedding.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Fly Away Home
programme.desc: Lisa gets cold feet on her wedding day, while Tia and Tamera entertain an anxious crowd waiting for the ceremony.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Meeting
programme.desc: Once separated at birth, identical twins Tia and Tamera, rediscover each other and scheme to live under one roof with their adoptive parents, who have nothing in common but their love for the girls.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: First Dates
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera vie for dates to the big school dance, while Ray and Lisa clash over the rules of the house.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Slumber Party
programme.desc: The boyfriend of one of Tia and Tamera's slumber party guests crashes the bash, while Lisa discovers that her beau is stepping out with another woman.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cheater Cheater
programme.desc: Tamera cajoles Tia into helping her cheat on a test in order to win Ray's approval.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Wedding Bells & Box Boys
programme.desc: Lisa blames Ray for leading a bridal client astray, while Tia and Tamera see Roger in a whole new light during a double date.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Out Alone
programme.desc: While taking a bus to the movies, Tia and Tamera fight over the disadvantages of being a twin, but when they get lost, they must team up to find their way home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Pimple
programme.desc: When a pimple pops up just before Tia's big date, Tamera agrees to step in for her -- and finds herself falling for Tia's guy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Car Trouble
programme.desc: Tamera scratches the fender of her father's prized car, then tries to hide the truth with the help of Tia and Roger.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Birthday
programme.desc: The twins' first birthday together leaves Tamera's best friend feeling left out, and Ray locked in an argument with Lisa over his parenting methods.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Love Strikes
programme.desc: Fearing they'll be separated forever if Ray and Lisa marry other people, Tia and Tamera plot to derail Ray's new romance.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Mothers & Other Strangers
programme.desc: When Lisa's disapproving mother comes for a visit, Ray agrees to pose as her husband.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Concert
programme.desc: After their parents forbid them to attend a rap concert, the twins sneak out of the house to meet their idol anyway.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Hair Today
programme.desc: Tamera's glamorous new look gets her into a snobby clique -- but threatens her relationship with Tia, who's been snubbed by the same crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Get a Job
programme.desc: Bored with their jobs at Ray's limousine company, the twins secretly start working at a burger stand and quickly learn the perils of trying to be in two places at the same time.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Joeys Choice
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera face "double trouble" when the same guy unknowingly asks them both out on the same date!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: A Tall Tale
programme.desc: Prom night could wind up being a date with disaster when Tia and Tamera's escorts behave badly, and Roger's date towers over him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Its a Love Thang
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Tamera, Lisa, and Ray all have close encounters of the romantic kind.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Free Billy
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera rescue a pig who's being mistreated by its owner and attempt to secretly stash the "porker" at home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister
programme.desc: While recovering from "twin appendectomies," Tia and Tamera go in search of hospital records that could reveal the identities of their biological parents.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Tattoo
programme.desc: When Lisa forbids Tia to see her boyfriend Michael, Tamera acts as a go-between for the couple -- but finds herself falling for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Two for the Road
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera take their sisterly bond a little too far when they collide with each other's cars while learning to drive.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Its A Party Thang
programme.desc: When the "cool crowd" doesn't invite Tia and Tamera to their "happening" party, the twins decide to throw a bash of their own -- which could wind up being a "bust"!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Field Trip
programme.desc: A class field trip to a museum lands the twins in detention, while Roger "borrows" a statue that makes him irresistible to women.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Put to the Test
programme.desc: When Tia and Tamera's SAT scores come in, A-student Tia is stunned to discover that Tamera has pulled a higher score than she has.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid in Play
programme.desc: After Tamera wins the lead in the school play, she transforms from a calm high school student into the ultimate "drama diva," who's driving everyone -- particularly Tia -- crazy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Dream Lover
programme.desc: When Tia confides in Tamera that she dreamed of kissing "Stinky Steve," their boss at Rocket Burger, Steve mistakenly believes she's got "the hots" for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Scrambled Eggs
programme.desc: A school project in which Tia and Tamera "marry" Michael and Roger proves to be an eye-opening experience for the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Smoking in the Girls Room
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera have a new "burning passion" -- for cigarettes.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Playing Hooky
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera are caught in the middle when Ray and Lisa square off over Lisa's decision to let the twins "play hooky" from school

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Single White Teenager
programme.desc: When Tia's out sick from school, Tamera befriends a new girl who becomes obsessed with looking and acting like the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Do?
programme.desc: Lisa gets "cold feet" after Terrence pops the question, while the twins worry that Lisa's marriage means they'll stop living under the same roof.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love Tubby Toast, and Teletubbies love children. So the Teletubbies stop to watch Ned working on his bicycle. Po finds a flag and leads Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa on a march around Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun sliding down into Home Hill. They watch some children painting circus pictures before the Lion and Bear appear in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A little boy goes for a ride on his Grandad’s motorbike and sidecar. In Teletubbyland, Po went to see Tinky Winky on her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po feels like having some Tubby Custard. The Teletubbies watch some children visiting a conservatory and drawing cacti. Tinky Winky goes for a walk. Five birds appear in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies dance round the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy & Laa-Laa hug and dance and then join the others to watch some children and Andy Brown play catching leaves in Autumn. T

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies jump out to say "Eh-oh" before watching a girl paint a picture of her dad. A voice trumpet rises and sings the nursery rhyme Looby Loo, the Tubbies join in and dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to say "Meow". The Tubbies watch three boys play with their new kittens. A voice trumpet rises and announces that it is 'Time to get up.' But the Teletubbies are too tired and go back to bed.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children looking through a telescope at the moon. In Teletubbyland, Po is going round and round.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It’s time for Tubby Toast. Dipsy and Laa-Laa are so excited, they bounce up and down on their seats so much they fall off. They sit up to watch some children watching some birds in a bird bath.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po counts to three. The Teletubbies watch Sam have a ride in a helicopter. Laa-Laa's ball gets stuck up a tree and three magic ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies join in with the game when a voice trumpet sings the Grand Old Duke of York. The magic windmill brings a musical transmission featuring King Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit a Sealife centre to look at seahorses. The Teletubbies go looking for interesting things. Tinky Winky sees his reflection in a puddle. Dipsy sees different clouds.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance around the trees and watch some children drum with their friend Norris. Tinky Winky dance on the roof of Home Hill and wakes up the other Tubbies.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some girls live with their Mum and Dad on a boat. They show us inside the boat. The Teletubbies come out of the house. Tinky Winky and Po go left and Dipsy and Laa-Laa go right.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl make some Bagels with her uncle. It’s time for Tubby Custard. Laa-Laa sits down to eat her Tubby Custard, but she waits for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and Amber look for some ladybirds. Tinky Winky, Dipsy & Laa-Laa decide to go high up. Tinky Winky walks to the top of the path.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy comes out of the house, but has to go back in to fetch the other Tubbies. They watch some children picking oranges. Laa-Laa plays with her ball indoors, but it gets stuck.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children climb a church tower to watch their mums ring the church bells. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po each sing a special song into Tinky Winky's bag, but when he drops his bag their songs make a lovely new mixed up song.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children go for a walk to find some red squirrels. The Teletubbies are playing a hiding game. Where are they? Can Noo-Noo find them?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po make some adjustments on the switch panel and laugh at the funny noises. The Tubbies watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty duck.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl and her sister getting ready to go away for a day. Tinky Winky puts all the Teletubbies favourite things into his bag.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy and Laa-Laa go for a walk and have fun saying hello to Tinky Winky and Po. The Teletubbies watch Alex Pascall share the story of the 'Owl Babies' with some children.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It’s very windy in Teletubbyland. The wind blows Tinky Winky’s skirt, Dipsy’s hat, Laa-Laa’s ball and Po off her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children looking at penguins waddling and swimming. In Teletubbyland, the Tubbies are walking. They walk fast and slow, far and near and other different ways.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies take turns to stand on a hill, in front of the house, next to a tree under a cloud. Andy Brown and some children visit some giraffes.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa comes out of the house does a little dance and calls the other Teletubbies for a Big Hug. They stop to watch 2 little girls making a pretend friend, out of old clothes.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are running around the house, but stop to see some children visit a level crossing and watch a train pass by. The Teletubbies play their Ready, Steady, Go game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children play a game with their fingers. A voice trumpet rises and sings the nursery rhyme ‘Ring-a-Roses’ to the Teletubbies.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are playing with their favourite things, they stop to watch an international Christmas insert. Lots of clouds appear in Teletubbyland and begin to snow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind a hill to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty sausage.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love dancing, but everything stops when the Teletubbies watch a little boy making drinking chocolate. There's time before Tubby Custard before three magic ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa makes some adjustments on the switch panel. The Teletubbies watch Sam and his mum blow some long horns.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and counts five. Tinky Winky jumps five times and some children learn about the number five.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up to say hello. The Teletubbies watch some children making hay. A tree appears in Teletubbyland and the Noo-Noo tidies up all the Teletubbies favourite things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have trouble getting out of bed. The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children feeding swans.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rose West
programme.desc: With her husband Fred, Rose West brutally murdered ten girls between 1973 and 1979, making her one of the most high profile serial killers in the UK.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Ann Maguire
programme.desc: The tragic story of Ann Maguire, who was stabbed to death by a teenage pupil while teaching a Spanish lesson at Corpus Christi Catholic College in Leeds.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The parents of innocent 11-year-old Rhys Jones, shot on his way home from a football club in Liverpool 2007, speak about the harrowing day when they lost their son.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jeremy Bamber
programme.desc: An investigation into Jeremy Bamber's brutal murder of his family in Essex, which occurred in 1985. Sentenced for life, he still maintains he is innocent. Is the right person behind bars?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Clare Wood
programme.desc: In April 2007, Clare Wood met George Appleton on a dating website and they embarked on a relationship together. Unbeknownst to her, Appleton was a criminal with a severe history of violence against women.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Manchester
programme.desc: The series returns to take a closer look at the most horrific crimes ever committed on British soil, starting with the May 2017 Manchester Arena bombing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Marchioness Disaster
programme.desc: The story of the Marchioness; a pleasure boat that collided with dredger Bowbelle and sank within minutes resulting in the loss of 51 lives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Jill Dando
programme.desc: Documentary examining the unsolved murder of British TV presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot outside her house in Fulham, London, in 1999.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Bad Timing
programme.desc: Joan begins to question her relationship with Sean when he gives her a power drill for Valentine's and doesn't hear from him for three days.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Old Dog
programme.desc: Joan decides it is time to give in on her three-month rule and she is finally going to have sex with her boyfriend Sean.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Declaration of Lynndependence
programme.desc: Joan and Toni decide that Lynn needs to learn to be independent. They give her a week to find a job, then she is on her own.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Friends, Colleagues, Brothers
programme.desc: Joan discovers that being the only black woman at her firm has many disadvantages and she is constantly ignored and overlooked.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Diss-regard
programme.desc: Maya checks into the hospital for her fibroid surgery and Joan has trouble managing her time between her boyfriend and her friends.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Burning Vagina Monologues
programme.desc: Joan is stressing because Sean hasn't told her that he loves her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Kiss Before Lying
programme.desc: Sean notices that Joan always flirts with Davis. He gets jealous and tells Joan that she can never see Davis again or go back to 847.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Loose Lips Sink Relationships
programme.desc: Joan opens her mouth and tells Greg about Toni and Clay.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Jamaic-Up?
programme.desc: Joan takes the girls to the Sinbad Music Festival to help ease Toni to get over her breakup with Greg.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Fallout
programme.desc: The ladies return home from their very eventful trip to Jamaica where Joan unintentionally ruined Toni's relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Say No
programme.desc: Joan continues to suffer in her troubled friendship with Toni and her new relationship with her therapist, Dr. Bales.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Full Court Conspiracy
programme.desc: Torn between her two worlds, Joan struggles with her professional duty while at the same time dealing with the guilt that she is not being true to herself or her beliefs.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Un-Treatable
programme.desc: Maya buys a dress that is too expensive knowing it might cause problems with her husband, Darnell. Joan treats herself to a trip to New York to visit her boyfriend, realizing the distance between the two of them is a problem.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Buh-Bye
programme.desc: Joan ends the friendship between her and Toni after finding out that Toni has been discussing her personal life with co-workers behind her back. Meanwhile, Lynn is trying to cope with her new living situation with Vosco, and his lack of a job.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He
programme.desc: Yvonne is concerned that William is never going to marry her because they have not set a date for the wedding. When Yvonne asks Joan for advice, Joan tries her best to stay out of it but ends up telling Yvonne to move in with William first to try it out.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Joan’s Birthday Suit
programme.desc: For her thirtieth birthday, Joan finds that she is happy with who she is, yet finds that she’s not so happy that her father is a nudist.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Trick or Truth?
programme.desc: When Toni loses her friend Joan, her man, and her gold card, it’s up to Maya and Lynn to help her get her life back together.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Maya Takes a Stan
programme.desc: Due to marital tensions at home, an emotionally fragile Maya finds herself falling into a fantasy affair with a handsome and empathetic new friend, Stan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Mom’s the Word
programme.desc: Toni meets a great man but is dumped after she’s a bad influence on his eight-year-old daughter. Joan dates a movie star but is dumped when he begins to think she’s stalking him.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Better Watch Out
programme.desc: Maya refuses to end her relationship with Stan, despite her girlfriends' repeated warnings to do so.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Have a Dream House
programme.desc: As a gesture of his love, William buys a beautiful house for his fiance, Yvonne.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sister, Sistah
programme.desc: Lynn's Caucasian sister, Tanya, travels to LA to help Lynn celebrate her Gotcha Day: the day when Lynn's childhood adoption to her Caucasian family became legal.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter
programme.desc: The week of William and Yvonne's wedding is finally here and all goes smoothly until Yvonne discovers William never told his mother about Yvonne...ever.
programme.sub-title: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter

programme.title: Girlfriends: Can’t Stan Ya
programme.desc: After a horrid kiss, Maya finally realizes she must end her relationship with Stan. Afterward, however, Stan deviously becomes good friends with Maya’s husband, just to make Maya squirm as he says he may or may not tell her husband about them.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Take Me Out After the Ball Game
programme.desc: As the company softball season kicks off, Joan falls for her new teammate: a young new employee from the mailroom; however, things go sour when she publicly dismisses their relationship due to their age difference.
programme.sub-title: Take Me Out After the Ball Game

programme.title: Girlfriends: Child’s in Charge
programme.desc: Toni decides to make something of her life by opening her own exclusive real estate agency after she sells a four-point-two million-dollar property.

programme.title: Girlfriends: X Does Not Mark the Spot
programme.desc: Joan becomes more adventurous with her boyfriend Chris, but inadvertently takes Ecstasy at a club with him and has a bad trip that puts her into the hospital.
programme.sub-title: X Does Not Mark the Spot

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Taming of the Realtess
programme.desc: Toni is frantic as the grand opening of her real estate agency is less than a week away.

programme.title: Girlfriends: My Mother, Myself
programme.desc: Just before Mother’s Day, Maya is disturbed when her mother announces she’s selling the house and moving to San Bernardino with her boyfriend. Toni has issues when her mother shows up unannounced for Mother’s Day and continually embarrasses her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Dessert
programme.desc: Unable to work out living with Joan, Lynn moves in with William and the two hit it off in more ways than one; becoming casual, unattached sex buddies.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Into the Woods
programme.desc: Joan dumps Chris when she learns her ex-boyfriend, Sean, is moving back to town; but is crushed when Sean lies about being engaged just to avoid being in a relationship with her again.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Getting Our Act Together
programme.desc: Maya dreams of reuniting with Darnell, Lynn becomes a bartender and William tries to fast-track his partnership at the law firm.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Handling Baggage
programme.desc: Maya struggles to save her marriage.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Coming to Terms
programme.desc: Eager to meet new men, Joan throws a "Bring Your Ex" party

programme.title: Girlfriends: Secrets and Eyes
programme.desc: Toni wrestles with growing older, and William makes a love connection.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Invasion of the Golddigger
programme.desc: Joan is not crazy about William’s new girlfriend, while Toni’s plastic surgeon comes up short on the dating front.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Don't Leave Me a Loan
programme.desc: While Maya tries to hide her marital woes from her mother, Lynn faces a financial reckoning with her parents

programme.title: Girlfriends: Star Craving Mad
programme.desc: Joan meets a movie star who isn’t as deep as he seems, and William’s sister makes a surprising request.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Blinded by the Lights
programme.desc: Joan discovers that dating a screen star isn’t as glamorous as it seems.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Mommy Returns
programme.desc: Lynn’s long-lost birth mother resurfaces after 30 years

programme.title: K-Everything
programme.desc: While today's generation aspire to look like flawless K-pop idols, Daniel Dae Kim hears how the desire to control appearance is deeply ingrained in Korean society, driven by rapid modernisation and political shifts.
